shared.title: Title
shared.description: A short description of the action that a user needs to complete, character count 120.
shared.label: Label
shared.cardDescription: Include a short description here that speaks to the value prop of the feature. Character limit of 120.

componentLibrary.title: Component Library
componentLibrary.subtitle: Living Design 3.5 Components
componentLibrary.overviewDescription: A comprehensive component library for Walmart. Each component follows the Living Design 3.5 specification with proper accessibility, semantic tokens, and responsive behavior.
componentLibrary.sandboxDescription: Interactive testing environment for Living Design 3.5 components. Select a component and adjust its properties in real-time.
componentLibrary.selectToConfig: Select a component to configure its properties
componentLibrary.gettingStartedDesc: Step-by-step setup instructions, project structure, dependencies, and everything you need to start building with the Living Design 3.5 Portable Kit.
componentLibrary.descBasicBanner: A value prop or branded banner with icon and text. Supports Default, Brand, and Inverse variants.
componentLibrary.quantityStepperDesc: Enable customers to increment or decrement the quantity of a specific item, supporting primary, secondary, and tertiary visual styles.
componentLibrary.descWCPHeartView: Favorites toggle button with desktop callout and mobile snackbar feedback
componentLibrary.descWCPRating: Interactive star rating component with labels for the WCP consumer experience
componentLibrary.descWCPSearchBar: WCP inline page search bar with two breakpoints and states: Enabled, Hovered, Activated, and Disabled
componentLibrary.descWCPSignatureCapture: WCP subscription signature flow with Trigger, Terms, Base form, and Reauth variants
componentLibrary.descWCPUploadImage: Photo upload field for customer reviews. Supports up to 5 images with mobile and desktop tile sizes.
componentLibrary.descWCPRichMediaSheet: Slot-driven, themeable bottom sheet for media-rich WCP experiences
componentLibrary.descWCPRichSnackbar: WCP snackbar with leading media slot and 4 color variants for product-level contextual messages
componentLibrary.descWCPTimerView: WCP countdown timer with urgency states for limited-time offers and queue reservations
componentLibrary.descWCPQueueBanner: WCP banner with live countdown for reserved cart items and limited-time queue flows
componentLibrary.descWCPQueueCard: WCP card showing reservation timer, product details, and queue actions across waiting, warning, and expiring states
componentLibrary.descWCPQueue: Combined Queue pattern page: Queue Landing, Queue Banner, and Queue Card
componentLibrary.descWCPUploadImage: Photo upload field for customer reviews supporting up to 5 images with responsive tile sizing
componentLibrary.descCarouselsGrids: Horizontally scrollable product carousels and category grids for the Walmart home page
componentLibrary.descOrderStatusCards: Active curbside, order status, and delivery status cards with countdown timers and actions
componentLibrary.descAlerts: Banner messages for info, success, warning, and error states
componentLibrary.descBadges: Count badges, status indicators, and semantic color variants
componentLibrary.descBreadcrumbs: Navigation breadcrumbs with support for 2-5 levels and custom separators
componentLibrary.descButtons: Primary, secondary, tertiary, and destructive button variants with full accessibility
componentLibrary.descCallouts: Contextual tooltips with directional arrows for onboarding
componentLibrary.descCards: Card containers with headers, actions, and content areas
componentLibrary.descCheckboxes: Single and grouped checkboxes with indeterminate state support
componentLibrary.descChips: Interactive, selectable buttons for categories and selections
componentLibrary.descContentMessages: Full-page state messages for errors, permissions, and loading
componentLibrary.descDateFields: Text input fields for date entry with validation
componentLibrary.descDatePickers: Calendar popup for visual date selection
componentLibrary.descDividers: Horizontal and vertical separators for content sections
componentLibrary.descFilterChips: Pill-shaped toggleable chips with counts for filtering
componentLibrary.descFormGroups: Fieldset containers for checkbox and radio groups
componentLibrary.descIconButtons: Icon-only buttons for compact actions with ghost, primary, secondary variants
componentLibrary.descIcons: Complete icon library with 100+ React components organized by category
componentLibrary.descLinkButtons: Link-styled interactive elements with icon support and multiple sizes
componentLibrary.descLinks: Text links with icon support and semantic color variants
componentLibrary.descLists: Vertical lists with leading icons and trailing content
componentLibrary.descMagicBox: AI-powered loading animation with sparkle effects
componentLibrary.descMenu: Dropdown menus with keyboard navigation and positioning
componentLibrary.descMetrics: Display critical data points with trend indicators and units
componentLibrary.descModals: Centered overlay dialogs with size variants for focused user interactions
componentLibrary.descNudges: Non-critical supportive information with actions and dismissible states
componentLibrary.descPanels: Slide-out panels for supplemental content and forms with three size variants
componentLibrary.descProgressIndicator: Circular or linear progress indicators for loading states
componentLibrary.descProgressTracker: Step-by-step progress visualization for multi-step processes and workflows
componentLibrary.descRadioButtons: Mutually exclusive selection within a group
componentLibrary.descSelect: Dropdown select component with error states, AI-assisted variant, and full accessibility
componentLibrary.descSnackbars: Brief feedback messages that appear at the bottom of the screen
componentLibrary.descSpinners: Loading indicators for indeterminate processes with color and size variants
componentLibrary.descSpotIcons: Decorative icon containers with brand and neutral color variants
componentLibrary.descSwitches: Toggle controls for binary on/off settings with immediate state changes
componentLibrary.descTabNavigation: Page-level navigation tabs with selected state and icon support
componentLibrary.descTags: Compact labels for categorization, status, and metadata
componentLibrary.descTextArea: Multi-line text input with character counting and AI-generated content indicators
componentLibrary.descTextFields: Single-line text inputs with labels, error states, helper text, and icons
componentLibrary.descBottomSheet: Mobile-friendly modal that slides up from the bottom with LD 3.5 tokens
componentLibrary.descSegmentedControl: Mutually exclusive tab-like controls for switching between views or options
componentLibrary.descQuantityStepper: Increment and decrement controls for item quantity selection
componentLibrary.descDateRangePicker: Calendar date range selector with start and end date inputs
componentLibrary.descOrderCardPatterns: Composable order card patterns for purchase history and order tracking
componentLibrary.descWCPCountry: Country picker bottom sheet, phone number input with country code selector, and country select group
componentLibrary.descWCPFloatingButton: Circular elevated icon button for carousel controls and floating action scenarios
componentLibrary.descTable: Structured data table with header, body, and footer
componentLibrary.descAlertDialog: Modal dialog for important confirmations that interrupt user workflow
componentLibrary.descAvatar: User profile image with fallback initials and status indicators
componentLibrary.descCalendar: Date picker calendar with single and range selection
componentLibrary.descCarousel: Horizontal scrolling content carousel with navigation controls
componentLibrary.descChart: Data visualization charts with Recharts and LD 3.5 tokens
componentLibrary.descCommand: Command palette for fast keyboard-driven search and navigation
componentLibrary.descContextMenu: Right-click context menu with submenus and keyboard navigation
componentLibrary.descDialog: Modal overlay dialog for focused interactions
componentLibrary.descDropdownMenu: Dropdown menu with submenus, checkbox, and radio items
componentLibrary.descForm: Form components with validation, error handling, and accessible labels
componentLibrary.descMenubar: Horizontal menu bar with dropdown menus and keyboard navigation
componentLibrary.descNavigationMenu: Top-level navigation with multi-column dropdown panels
componentLibrary.descPagination: Navigation controls for paging through large data sets
componentLibrary.descPopover: Floating popover panel with arrow indicator
componentLibrary.descProgress: Horizontal progress bar for determinate operations
componentLibrary.descRadioGroup: Mutually exclusive radio buttons with Radix primitives
componentLibrary.descScrollArea: Custom scrollable container with styled scrollbars
componentLibrary.descSeparator: Visual divider in horizontal or vertical orientation
componentLibrary.descSheet: Side sheet overlay for supplemental content
componentLibrary.descSlider: Draggable slider for selecting numeric values within a range
componentLibrary.descSwitch: Toggle switch built with Radix primitives
componentLibrary.descToggle: Two-state button for toolbar actions and view modes
componentLibrary.descWCPFlag: Product badge labels for Clearance, Bestseller, Rollback, and promotional flags
componentLibrary.descWCPRating: Star rating display component with half-star support and review count
componentLibrary.descWCPButtonGroups: Grouped button patterns for filter bars, toolbars, and action sets
componentLibrary.descDropdowns: Dropdown trigger patterns for account, reorder, and department menus
componentLibrary.descSearchUtility: Search input with utility bar patterns for desktop and mobile
componentLibrary.descWCPRichMediaSheet: Full-screen dark media overlay for rich product imagery and video
componentLibrary.descItemTile: Product tile component for carousels and grids with price, image, and actions
componentLibrary.descFooterPatterns: Page footer layout patterns with navigation links and legal content
componentLibrary.descTopNav: Top navigation bar patterns for desktop header and mobile top nav
componentLibrary.descPageTemplates: Full page layout templates combining nav, content, and footer shells
componentLibrary.descSideNav: Side navigation panel with collapsible sections and active state tracking
componentLibrary.descSearchResults: Search results page pattern with filters, sorting, and product grid
componentLibrary.descHomePageWidgets: Homepage section widgets including hero banners, product rows, and promos
componentLibrary.descPromoBanners: Promotional banner patterns for sales, seasonal events, and announcements
componentLibrary.descCarouselsGrids: Horizontal scroll carousels and responsive product grid layouts
componentLibrary.descOrderStatusCards: Order tracking and delivery status card patterns
componentLibrary.buttonsPageDesc: Primary, secondary, tertiary, and destructive button variants with full accessibility support. All buttons use Living Design 3.5 semantic tokens and support multiple sizes.
componentLibrary.ratingNote: Rating is display-only (not interactive). Shows 0-5 stars with half-star support.
componentLibrary.interactiveExamplesDesc: Click-triggered callouts anchored to buttons, demonstrating real-world usage patterns.
componentLibrary.drawerReplacedDesc: The SHADCN/Radix Drawer component has been replaced with BottomSheet from Living Design 3.5. The example below demonstrates the new BottomSheet component.
componentLibrary.labelsDesc: TextField includes integrated labels. For standalone labels (e.g., with checkboxes), use native HTML label elements.
componentLibrary.textFieldsPageDesc: Single-line text inputs with built-in labels, error states, helper text, leading icons, trailing content, and AI-generated content indicators. Text fields support multiple sizes, input types, and accessibility features. This component also replaces the previous standalone Label component.
componentLibrary.popoverWhenToUse1: Providing relevant supporting information or actions
componentLibrary.popoverWhenToUse2: Presenting a short list of options for the current view
componentLibrary.popoverWhenNotToUse1: Displaying complex information or multi-step flows (use Modal instead)
componentLibrary.popoverWhenNotToUse2: Making selections (use Select or radio group instead)
componentLibrary.popoverWhenNotToUse3: Showing content on page load without user interaction
componentLibrary.popoverA11y1: Trigger must be keyboard-focusable with accessible name
componentLibrary.popoverA11y2: Focus moves into popover on open and returns to trigger on close
componentLibrary.popoverA11y3: Supports Escape key and outside click to dismiss
componentLibrary.popoverA11y4: Tab and Shift+Tab navigate through focusable content
componentLibrary.progressWhenToUse1: Showing progress of file uploads or downloads
componentLibrary.progressWhenToUse3: Indicating system resource usage (storage, memory, etc.)
componentLibrary.progressWhenToUse5: Showing loading or processing states with known duration
componentLibrary.progressWhenNotToUse1: For indeterminate loading states (use Spinner instead)
componentLibrary.progressWhenNotToUse2: For stepper navigation (use Stepper component instead)
componentLibrary.progressWhenNotToUse3: As a decorative element without meaningful progress data
componentLibrary.progressVariantPrimary: Default variant for general progress indication
componentLibrary.progressVariantSuccess: Use when progress indicates successful completion or positive state
componentLibrary.progressVariantWarning: Use when progress is in a cautionary state (e.g., storage almost full)
componentLibrary.progressVariantError: Use when progress indicates a critical or error state
componentLibrary.progressA11y1: Uses role="progressbar" for screen reader support
componentLibrary.progressA11y2: Includes aria-valuenow, aria-valuemin, and aria-valuemax attributes
componentLibrary.progressA11y3: Label prop provides accessible name via aria-label
componentLibrary.progressA11y4: Color variants must not be the sole indicator of status (always include labels)
componentLibrary.progressA11y5: Sufficient color contrast between fill and background (WCAG AA compliant)
componentLibrary.progressTokensDesc: This component uses Living Design 3.5 semantic tokens for consistent styling:
componentLibrary.progressLabelDesc: Label displayed on the left side
componentLibrary.progressValueLabelDesc: Custom label displayed on the right side
componentLibrary.trackerWhenToUse2: Checkout processes (e.g., Cart → Shipping → Payment → Review)
componentLibrary.trackerWhenToUse7: Any process with discrete, sequential steps
componentLibrary.trackerWhenNotToUse1: For continuous progress (0-100%) - use ProgressIndicator instead
componentLibrary.trackerWhenNotToUse2: For file uploads or downloads - use ProgressIndicator instead
componentLibrary.trackerWhenNotToUse3: For loading states - use ProgressIndicator or Spinner instead
componentLibrary.trackerWhenNotToUse4: For processes without clear, distinct steps
componentLibrary.trackerStepCompleted: Steps before the active step (filled circle in status color)
componentLibrary.trackerStepActive: Current step (double-circle indicator in status color)
componentLibrary.trackerStepFuture: Steps after the active step (filled circle in gray)
componentLibrary.trackerBest1: Keep labels short and clear (1-3 words)
componentLibrary.trackerBest3: Choose status based on process state
componentLibrary.trackerBest4: Ensure steps are sequential and logical
componentLibrary.trackerA11y2: Ensure parent context provides accessible announcements for step changes
componentLibrary.trackerA11y3: Consider using aria-live region in parent component for dynamic updates
componentLibrary.trackerA11y4: Labels should be clear and concise for screen readers
componentLibrary.trackerA11y5: Color alone should not convey information - use labels
componentLibrary.trackerTokensDesc: This component uses Living Design 3.5 semantic tokens exclusively:
componentLibrary.trackerRelatedButtonGroup: Often used with Progress Tracker for step navigation
componentLibrary.selectWhenToUse1: When users need to choose a single option from 5-15 options
componentLibrary.selectWhenToUse2: For forms where screen space is limited
componentLibrary.selectWhenToUse3: When options need to be organized into groups
componentLibrary.selectWhenNotToUse1: For 2-4 options, use radio buttons instead for better visibility
componentLibrary.selectWhenNotToUse2: For multiple selections, use checkboxes or a multi-select component
componentLibrary.selectWhenNotToUse3: For more than 15 options, consider using an autocomplete/combobox
componentLibrary.selectA11y1: Fully keyboard navigable (Tab, Enter, Arrow keys)
componentLibrary.selectA11y2: ARIA labels and descriptions for screen readers
componentLibrary.selectA11y3: Error states announced to assistive technologies
componentLibrary.selectA11y4: Focus indicators meet WCAG 2.1 AA standards
componentLibrary.selectBest1: Always provide a clear, descriptive label
componentLibrary.selectBest2: Use error messages that explain how to fix the issue
componentLibrary.selectBest3: Provide helpful helper text for complex or important fields
componentLibrary.selectBest4: Use the magic variant sparingly for AI-powered features
componentLibrary.selectBest5: Group related options together for easier scanning
componentLibrary.selectBest6: Use the small size for compact layouts (typically desktop)
componentLibrary.selectBest7: Use the large size (default) for most use cases
componentLibrary.tabsWhenToUse1: For page-level navigation between different content views
componentLibrary.tabsWhenToUse2: When you need to show/hide large sections of related content
componentLibrary.tabsWhenToUse3: To organize content that doesn't fit in a single view
componentLibrary.tabsWhenToUse4: For filtering data tables or lists (e.g., "Active" vs "Archived")
componentLibrary.tabsBest1: Keep tab labels short and descriptive (1-2 words preferred)
componentLibrary.tabsBest2: Use 2-5 tabs maximum for optimal usability
componentLibrary.tabsBest3: Enable smallScreen mode for mobile-responsive layouts
componentLibrary.tabsBest4: Use trailing badges to show counts or status indicators
componentLibrary.tabsBest6: Use TabPanel to properly associate content with each tab
componentLibrary.tabsA11y1: Tabs support keyboard navigation (Tab, Arrow keys, Enter)
componentLibrary.tabsA11y2: Active tab is indicated with aria-selected="true"
componentLibrary.tabsA11y3: Each tab panel has role="tabpanel" for screen readers
componentLibrary.tabsA11y4: Focus states are clearly visible with LD 3.5 focus rings
componentLibrary.tabsA11y5: Disabled tabs are announced properly to assistive technology
componentLibrary.themesDesc: Switch between brand themes and explore all Living Design 3.5 design tokens. All components use these semantic tokens to ensure consistent theming across the application.
componentLibrary.themesExplanation: Themes are complete sets of design tokens that define the visual appearance of components. When you switch themes, all components automatically update to match the new color palette and typography.
componentLibrary.iconsDesc: Complete icon library with {{count}}+ React components organized by category. All icons support customizable size and color properties.
componentLibrary.guidelinesDesc: Design principles, best practices, and usage guidelines for Living Design 3.5 components.
componentLibrary.designTokensDesc: CSS custom properties for colors, spacing, typography, and other design values. Click any token to copy its CSS variable to clipboard.
